title: Pin-operator: precise compile-time vs runtime distinction across a mixed query
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.mixed_pin_query/2` that takes `user_id` and `min_score`, uses a module attribute `@allowed_statuses ["published", "featured"]` that SHOULD NOT be pinned (for query-plan performance), and filters: `p.user_id == ^user_id` (runtime, pinned), `p.score >= ^min_score` (runtime, pinned), `p.status in @allowed_statuses` (compile-time, NOT pinned so PostgreSQL can inline the list literal and pick a selective index). Add a code comment explaining why the status list is NOT pinned. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing BOTH sides of the pin rule — runtime = pin, compile-time = no pin, and the PG query plan reason. Subtle and rare.
research_citation: hubertlepicki: 'The change to not using the pin operator has a profound overall impact on the performance'
